Book of ‘The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 583

will say, ‘Intercede for us to your Lord, that He judges between us.’ He will say, ‘I am not the one for you, for I killed someone, but not out of retribution for the killing of another person. And nothing concerns me today except for my own self. But go to ‘Besa, a spirit (Roch) created by Allah and His Word (“Be!’” — and he was).’ So they go to ‘Eesa and say, ‘Intercede for us to our Lord, that He judges between us.’ He will say, ‘I am not the one for you. I was taken as a god besides Allah, and nothing concerns me (at this time) except my own self, But suppose that possessions were in a container that was sealed — could one get to what is on the inside without breaking the seal?’ They will say, ‘No.’ He will say, ‘Indeed, Muhammad is the seal of the Prophets, and he is present today. And his previous and later sins were forgiven for him.”

The Messenger of Allah #¥ said, “Then they will come to me and say, ‘O Muhammad, intercede for us to your Lord, that He judges between us.’ I will say, ‘Iam for it,’ until Allah gives permission for whomsoever He wishes and is pleased with. When Allah wills to separate between His creation, a caller will call out, ‘Where is Ahmad and his nation? We are the last ones and the first ones — the last of the nations and the first whose account will be taken. The nations will make way for us, and we will go forward, Ghurr (whiteness in the forehead; the faces of the believers will shine on the Day of Resurrection), Muhajjaloon (the places they wash in their Wudoo, ablution, will shine) from the effects of the Wudoo (ablution). And it will be said, ‘It almost seems as if all of the members of this nation are Prophets.’ And. I will go to the Door of Paradise. Then 1 will take the ring of the door and knock on the door. It will be said, ‘Who are you?’ Then I will say, ‘I am Muhammad.’ It will be opened, and I will see my Lord ‘Azza We-Jall (To Him belongs Might and Majesty) and He is on His Kursee (Footstool) or on His Bed (the uncertainty here is from the narrator, Hammnaad). | will fall down, prostrating to Him. And I will praise him with such praises that none before me praised Him with and none after me will praise Him with, Then it will be said, ‘O Muhammad, raise your head and ask, you will be given; speak, you will be heard; and intercede, your intercession will be granted.’ Then I will raise my hieact and say, ‘O my Lord, my nation, my nation,’ He #8 will say, ‘Take out he who has (Eemann) in his heart equal to the weight of such and such (Hammaad did not memorize the amount).’ ‘Then I will return and perform prostration. Then I will say as I said before, and He will

584 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

say, ‘Raise your head and speak, you will be heard; ask, you will be given; and intercede, your intercession will be granted,’ I will say, ‘O my Lord, my nation, my nation.’ He will say, ‘Take out he who has (Eemaan) in his heart equal to the weight of such and such,’ an amount that was less than the amount of the first time. Then I will return and I will perform prostration and I will say as 1 said before. it will be said to me, ‘Raise your head and speak, you will be heard, and intercede, your intercession will be granted.’ I will say, ‘My Lord, my nation, my nation,’ He will say, ‘Take out he who has (Femaan) in his heart equal to the weight of such and such,’ an amount that is even Jess than the amount of the previous time.” (Ahmad) Ton Maajah related parts of it. And we have already mentioned in the second and third categories of intercession those people who are ordered to the Hellfire, and then intercession is made so that they do not enter it.

The Narration Of ‘Abdullah Ibn ‘Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab

(here, parts are missing from the original manuscript until the next section).

In another narration, Ibn ‘Abbaas x related that the Messenger of Allah ig said, “My intercession is for the perpetrators, from my nation, of the major sins.” At-Tabaraanee related this in his Mo‘jam Al-Kabeer.

‘Abdullah ibn ‘Umar . reported that the Prophet @: said, “I was given a choice between intercession and between half of my nation being in Paradise, and 1 chose intercession, because it is more comprehensive and more sufficient. Do you see that it is for the Muttageen (the pious, the righteous, etc.)? No, but rather it is for the stained, the sinners.” (Ahmad) Tbn Abeo Ad-Dunyaa related the same through a different chain. And I found the same in Kifaab AlAluwvaal, And Al-Baihagee related it in Al-Ba'th Wen-Nushoor through a different chain of narrators.

The Narration Of ‘Abdullah Ibn ‘Amr Ibn Al-‘Aas . ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Amr ibn, Al-’Aas # reported thai the Messenger of

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 585

Allah ig recited the saying of Allah which recounts the saying of Tbraaheem 342i: Bo BIL ae SiS Deb oie

“O my Lord! They have indeed led astray many among mankind. But whoso follows me, he verily is of me. And whoso disobeys me, still You are indeed Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.’

And the saying of Allah % which recounts the saying of ‘Eesa 2: pa poe Hh Che ode ote ye Fae ose ort

And the Saying of Allah % which recounts the saying of Noah sé: 455 Gal & ANE BIS G SGP

“My Lord! Leave not one of the disbelievers on the earth!?l

‘The Narration Of ‘Abdullah Ibn Mas’ood 2

We have hitherto mentioned the narration of ‘Ilgimah about the Haid (the Basin), the station of praise and glory, and in it, the intercession is mentioned as well.

586 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

The Narration Of ‘Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn Abee “‘Ageel 2s

“Abdur-Rahmaan ibn Abee ‘Aqeel a said, “I went with a delegation to the Prophet #8, and when we reached him, we made our (camels) kneel at (his) door. There was no person who was more despised by us than the man we were entering upon, and when we came out, there was no man more beloved to us than the man we had just entered upon, One among them (among the delegation) said, ‘O Messenger of Allah, you asked your Lord for the dominion of Sulaimaan?’ The Messenger of Allah ## laughed and then said, ‘Perhaps to fulfill your needs is better with Allah than the dominion of Sulaimaan. Verily, Allah did not send a Prophet except that He gave him a supplication. Among them was he who used it for the world, and he was given it. Among them was he who supplicated against his people after they disobeyed him, and they were destroyed by it. And indeed, Allah gave me a supplication, and I saved it with my Lord: intercession for my nation on the Day of Resurrection.” (Related by Al-Baihagee) The chain is Ghareeb and the hadith is Ghareeb.

The Narration Of The Leader Of The Believers, ‘Uthmaan Ibn ‘Affaan <&: The Intercessors On The Day Of Resurrection Are The Prophets, Then The Scholars, And Then The Martyrs

The Narration Of ‘Alee Ibn Abee Taalib PS

Harb ibn Shuraih Al-Bazzaar said, “I said to Abee Ja’far Muhammad ibn ‘Alee, ‘Do you see this intercession that the people of Iraq are talking about, is it true?’ He said, ‘Intercession of what?’ I said, ‘The intercession of Muhammad #5. He said, ‘Yes, by Allah, it is true. By Allah, my uncle, Muhammad ibn ‘Alee ibn Al-

Book of The End : Great Trials and Tribulations 587

Hanafiyyah related to me from ‘Alee 4 that the Messenger of Allah #8 said: 1 will intercede for my nation until my Lord ‘Azza Wa-Jall (To Him belongs Might and Majesty) calls me and says ~ are you pleased, O Muhammad? Then I will say: My Lord, I am pleased.” (Related by Abu Bakr Al-Bazzaar) Al-Bazzaar then said, “We do not know of this being related except by this chain.”

The Narration Of ‘Aouf Ibn Maalik

“Aout ibn Maalik Al-Ashja’ee 4 related that the Messenger of Allah 28 said, “A comer from my Lord came to me in the night, giving me the choice between half of my nation entering Paradise and between intercession. And I chose intercession... 1 make those who are present to bear witness — that my intercession is for he, from my nation, who dies, not associating any partner whatsoever with, Allah.” (Related by Abu Bakr ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa)

Jn another narration, ‘Aouf ibn Maalik related that the Prophet # said, “Jibreel 3421 came to me from my Lord, and gave me a choice between two qualities: that half of my nation enter Paradise or the intercession, and I chose the intercession.” (Related by Ya’qoob ibn. Sufyaa) Al-Baihagee and Hammaad ibn Zaid both related it through their own respective chains.

The Narration Of Ka’ab Ibn ‘Ujrah 2%

Ka’ab ibn ‘Ujrah & said, “I said, ‘O Messenger of Allah, the intercession, the intercession.’ Then he # said, ‘My intercession is for the perpetrators, from my nation, of the major sins.” (Related by Al-Baihagee)

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 583

akan berkata, 'Berilah syafaat untuk kami kepada Tuhanmu, agar Dia menghakimi di antara kami.' Dia akan berkata, 'Aku bukan orangnya untuk itu, karena aku pernah membunuh seseorang, bukan sebagai balasan atas pembunuhan orang lain. Dan tidak ada yang mengkhawatirkanku hari ini kecuali diriku sendiri. Tetapi pergilah kepada 'Eesa, seorang ruh (Roch) yang diciptakan oleh Allah dan firman-Nya ("Jadilah!" — maka jadilah dia).' Maka mereka pergi kepada 'Eesa dan berkata, 'Berilah syafaat untuk kami kepada Tuhan kami, agar Dia menghakimi di antara kami.' Dia akan berkata, 'Aku bukan orangnya untuk itu. Aku pernah dijadikan tuhan selain Allah, dan tidak ada yang mengkhawatirkanku (saat ini) kecuali diriku sendiri. Tetapi bayangkan jika harta benda berada dalam wadah yang tersegel — dapatkah seseorang mencapai apa yang ada di dalamnya tanpa mematahkan segel?' Mereka akan berkata, 'Tidak.' Dia akan berkata, 'Sesungguhnya, Muhammad adalah penutup para Nabi, dan dia hadir hari ini. Dan dosa-dosanya yang terdahulu dan yang kemudian telah diampuni untuknya.'

Rasulullah #¥ bersabda, "Kemudian mereka akan datang kepadaku dan berkata, 'Wahai Muhammad, berilah syafaat untuk kami kepada Tuhanmu, agar Dia menghakimi di antara kami.' Aku akan berkata, 'Aku siap untuk itu,' hingga Allah mengizinkan siapa yang Dia kehendaki dan ridhai. Ketika Allah berkehendak untuk memisahkan di antara makhluk-Nya, seorang penyeru akan berseru, 'Di manakah Ahmad dan umatnya? Kami adalah yang terakhir dan yang pertama — yang terakhir dari umat-umat dan yang pertama dihisab. Umat-umat akan memberi jalan untuk kami, dan kami akan maju, Ghurr (putih di dahi; wajah para mukmin akan bercahaya pada Hari Kebangkitan), Muhajjaloon (tempat-tempat yang mereka basuh dalam wudu mereka akan bercahaya) dari bekas wudu. Dan akan dikatakan, 'Seolah-olah semua anggota umat ini adalah para Nabi.' Dan aku akan pergi ke Pintu Surga. Kemudian aku akan memegang cincin pintu dan mengetuk pintu. Akan dikatakan, 'Siapakah kamu?' Kemudian aku akan berkata, 'Aku adalah Muhammad.' Pintu akan dibuka, dan aku akan melihat Tuhanku 'Azza Wa-Jall (Bagi-Nya kemuliaan dan keagungan) dan Dia berada di atas Kursi-Nya (Takhta Kaki-Nya) atau di atas Tempat Tidur-Nya (ketidakpastian di sini berasal dari perawi, Hammaad). Aku akan terjatuh, bersujud kepada-Nya. Dan aku akan memuji-Nya dengan pujian yang tidak pernah dipanjatkan oleh siapa pun sebelumku dan tidak akan dipanjatkan oleh siapa pun setelahku. Kemudian akan dikatakan, 'Wahai Muhammad, angkatlah kepalamu dan mintalah, engkau akan diberi; berbicaralah, engkau akan didengar; dan berilah syafaat, syafaatmu akan diterima.' Kemudian aku akan mengangkat kepalaku dan berkata, 'Ya Tuhanku, umatku, umatku.' Dia #8 akan berkata, 'Keluarkanlah dia yang memiliki (iman) di hatinya seberat sekian dan sekian (Hammaad tidak mengingat jumlahnya).' Kemudian aku akan kembali dan bersujud. Kemudian aku akan berkata seperti yang aku katakan sebelumnya, dan Dia akan

584 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

berkata, 'Angkatlah kepalamu dan berbicaralah, engkau akan didengar; mintalah, engkau akan diberi; dan berilah syafaat, syafaatmu akan diterima.' Aku akan berkata, 'Ya Tuhanku, umatku, umatku.' Dia akan berkata, 'Keluarkanlah dia yang memiliki (iman) di hatinya seberat sekian dan sekian,' suatu jumlah yang lebih sedikit dari jumlah pertama. Kemudian aku akan kembali dan bersujud dan aku akan berkata seperti yang aku katakan sebelumnya. Akan dikatakan kepadaku, 'Angkatlah kepalamu dan berbicaralah, engkau akan didengar, dan berilah syafaat, syafaatmu akan diterima.' Aku akan berkata, 'Tuhanku, umatku, umatku.' Dia akan berkata, 'Keluarkanlah dia yang memiliki (iman) di hatinya seberat sekian dan sekian,' suatu jumlah yang bahkan lebih sedikit dari jumlah sebelumnya." (Ahmad) Ibnu Maajah meriwayatkan sebagian darinya. Dan kami telah menyebutkan dalam kategori syafaat kedua dan ketiga orang-orang yang diperintahkan masuk Neraka, kemudian syafaat diberikan sehingga mereka tidak memasukinya.

Riwayat 'Abdullah Ibn 'Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab

(di sini, ada bagian yang hilang dari manuskrip asli hingga bagian berikutnya).

Dalam riwayat lain, Ibnu 'Abbaas رضي الله عنه meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Syafaatku adalah untuk pelaku dosa-dosa besar dari umatku." At-Tabaraanee meriwayatkan ini dalam Mo'jam Al-Kabeer.

'Abdullah ibn 'Umar رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Aku diberi pilihan antara syafaat dan antara setengah dari umatku berada di Surga, dan aku memilih syafaat, karena ia lebih menyeluruh dan lebih mencukupi. Apakah engkau melihat bahwa itu hanya untuk orang-orang Muttaqeen (orang-orang yang bertakwa)? Tidak, melainkan untuk orang-orang yang berdosa, para pendosa." (Ahmad) Ibnu Abee Ad-Dunyaa meriwayatkan hal yang sama melalui rantai yang berbeda. Dan aku menemukan hal yang sama dalam Kitab Al-Ahwaal. Dan Al-Baihagee meriwayatkannya dalam Al-Ba'th Wan-Nushoor melalui rantai perawi yang berbeda.

Riwayat 'Abdullah Ibn 'Amr Ibn Al-'Aas رضي الله عنه. 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn Al-'Aas رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 585

صلى الله عليه وسلم membacakan firman Allah yang menceritakan ucapan Ibrahim عليه السلام: "Ya Tuhanku! Sesungguhnya berhala-berhala itu telah menyesatkan kebanyakan manusia. Barang siapa mengikutiku, maka sesungguhnya dia adalah dari golonganku. Dan barang siapa yang mendurhakaiku, maka sesungguhnya Engkau Maha Pengampun lagi Maha Penyayang.'

Dan firman Allah عز وجل yang menceritakan ucapan 'Eesa عليه السلام: "Jika Engkau menyiksa mereka, maka sesungguhnya mereka adalah hamba-hamba-Mu, dan jika Engkau mengampuni mereka, maka sesungguhnya Engkaulah Yang Maha Perkasa lagi Maha Bijaksana."

Dan firman Allah عز وجل yang menceritakan ucapan Nuh عليه السلام: "Ya Tuhanku! Janganlah Engkau biarkan seorang pun di antara orang-orang kafir itu tinggal di atas bumi!"

Riwayat 'Abdullah Ibn Mas'ood رضي الله عنه

Kami telah menyebutkan sebelumnya riwayat 'Alqamah tentang Al-Haud (Telaga), kedudukan pujian dan kemuliaan, dan di dalamnya, syafaat juga disebutkan.

586 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

Riwayat 'Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn Abee 'Aqeel رضي الله عنه

'Abdur-Rahmaan ibn Abee 'Aqeel رضي الله عنه berkata, "Aku pergi bersama delegasi kepada Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم, dan ketika kami tiba, kami menghentikan (unta-unta kami) di (pintu) nya. Tidak ada orang yang lebih kami benci daripada orang yang akan kami masuki, dan ketika kami keluar, tidak ada orang yang lebih kami cintai daripada orang yang baru saja kami masuki. Salah satu dari mereka (dari delegasi) berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, engkau meminta kepada Tuhanmu kerajaan Sulaiman?' Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم tertawa kemudian berkata, 'Barangkali memenuhi kebutuhanmu lebih baik di sisi Allah daripada kerajaan Sulaiman. Sesungguhnya Allah tidak mengutus seorang Nabi pun kecuali Dia memberikan kepadanya suatu doa yang dikabulkan. Di antara mereka ada yang menggunakannya untuk dunia, dan diberikan kepadanya. Di antara mereka ada yang berdoa mencelaka kaumnya setelah mereka mendurhakai-Nya, dan mereka dihancurkan olehnya. Dan sesungguhnya Allah memberiku suatu doa, dan aku menyimpannya di sisi Tuhanku: syafaat untuk umatku pada Hari Kiamat." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Baihagee) Sanadnya Gharib dan hadisnya Gharib.

Riwayat Pemimpin Kaum Mukminin, 'Utsmaan Ibn 'Affaan رضي الله عنه: Para Pemberi Syafaat pada Hari Kiamat Adalah Para Nabi, Kemudian Para Ulama, dan Kemudian Para Syuhada

Riwayat 'Ali Ibn Abee Taalib رضي الله عنه

Harb ibn Shuraih Al-Bazzaar berkata, "Aku berkata kepada Abee Ja'far Muhammad ibn 'Alee, 'Apakah pendapatmu tentang syafaat yang dibicarakan oleh penduduk Iraq, apakah itu benar?' Dia berkata, 'Syafaat apa?' Aku berkata, 'Syafaat Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم.' Dia berkata, 'Ya, demi Allah, itu benar. Demi Allah, pamanku, Muhammad ibn 'Alee ibn Al-

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 587

Hanafiyyah meriwayatkan kepadaku dari 'Ali رضي الله عنه bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda: Aku akan memberi syafaat untuk umatku hingga Tuhanku 'Azza Wa-Jall memanggil aku dan berkata — apakah engkau ridha, wahai Muhammad? Kemudian aku akan berkata: Tuhanku, aku ridha." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Bakr Al-Bazzaar) Al-Bazzaar kemudian berkata, "Kami tidak mengetahui ini diriwayatkan kecuali melalui rantai ini."

Riwayat 'Aouf Ibn Maalik

'Aouf ibn Maalik Al-Ashja'ee رضي الله عنه meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Seorang utusan dari Tuhanku datang kepadaku di malam hari, memberiku pilihan antara setengah dari umatku masuk Surga dan antara syafaat. Dan aku memilih syafaat... Aku membuat mereka yang hadir bersaksi — bahwa syafaatku adalah untuk dia, dari umatku, yang meninggal, tidak menyekutukan Allah dengan sesuatu apa pun." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Bakr ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa)

Dalam riwayat lain, 'Aouf ibn Maalik meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Jibril عليه السلام datang kepadaku dari Tuhanku, dan memberiku pilihan antara dua hal: bahwa setengah dari umatku masuk Surga atau syafaat, dan aku memilih syafaat." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ya'qoob ibn Sufyaan) Al-Baihagee dan Hammaad ibn Zaid keduanya meriwayatkannya melalui rantai masing-masing.

Riwayat Ka'ab Ibn 'Ujrah رضي الله عنه

Ka'ab ibn 'Ujrah رضي الله عنه berkata, "Aku berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, syafaat, syafaat.' Kemudian beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, 'Syafaatku adalah untuk pelaku dosa-dosa besar dari umatku.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Baihagee)
content_ar: الكتاب of ‘The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 583 will say, ‘Intercede for us to your Lord, that He judges between us.’ He will say, ‘I am not the one for you, for I killed someone, but not out of retribution for the killing of another person. And nothing concerns me today except for my own self. But go to ‘Besa, a الروح (Roch) created by الله and His Word (“Be!’” — and he was).’ لذا they go to ‘Eesa and say, ‘Intercede for us to our Lord, that He judges between us.’ He will say, ‘I am not the one for you. I was taken as a god بالإضافة إلى الله, and nothing concerns me (at this time) except my own self, But suppose that possessions were in a container that was sealed — could one get to what is on the inside without breaking the seal?’ They will say, ‘No.’ He will say, ‘Indeed, محمد ﷺ is the seal of the الأنبياء, and he is present today. And his previous and later الذنوب were forgiven for him.” الرسول ﷺ of الله #¥ said, “Then they will come to me and say, ‘O محمد ﷺ, intercede for us to your Lord, that He judges between us.’ I will say, ‘Iam for it,’ حتى الله gives permission for whomsoever He wishes and is pleased with. عندما الله wills to separate between His creation, a caller will call out, ‘Where is أحمد and his nation? We are the last ones and the أولاً ones — the last of the nations and the أولاً whose account will be taken. The nations will make الطريق for us, and we will go forward, Ghurr (whiteness in the forehead; the faces of the المؤمنون will shine on the Day of البعث), Muhajjaloon (the places they wash in their الوضوء, ablution, will shine) from the effects of the الوضوء (ablution). And it will be said, ‘It almost seems as إذا all of the members of this nation are الأنبياء.’ And. I will go to the Door of الجنة. Then 1 will take the ring of the door and knock on the door. It will be said, ‘Who are you?’ Then I will say, ‘I am محمد ﷺ.’ It will be opened, and I will see my Lord ‘Azza We-Jall (To Him belongs Might and Majesty) and He is on His Kursee (Footstool) or on His Bed (the uncertainty here is from the narrator, Hammnaad). | will fall down, prostrating to Him. And I will praise him with such praises that none قبل me praised Him with and none بعد me will praise Him with, Then it will be said, ‘O محمد ﷺ, raise your head and ask, you will be given; speak, you will be heard; and intercede, your intercession will be granted.’ Then I will raise my hieact and say, ‘O my Lord, my nation, my nation,’ He #8 will say, ‘Take out he who has (Eemann) in his القلب equal to the weight of such and such (Hammaad did not memorize the amount).’ ‘Then I will return and perform prostration. Then I will say as I said قبل, and He will 584 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations say, ‘Raise your head and speak, you will be heard; ask, you will be given; and intercede, your intercession will be granted,’ I will say, ‘O my Lord, my nation, my nation.’ He will say, ‘Take out he who has (Eemaan) in his القلب equal to the weight of such and such,’ an amount that was less than the amount of the أولاً time. Then I will return and I will perform prostration and I will say as 1 said قبل. it will be said to me, ‘Raise your head and speak, you will be heard, and intercede, your intercession will be granted.’ I will say, ‘My Lord, my nation, my nation,’ He will say, ‘Take out he who has (Femaan) in his القلب equal to the weight of such and such,’ an amount that is even Jess than the amount of the previous time.” (أحمد) Ton Maajah related parts of it. And we have already mentioned in the ثانياً and ثالثاً categories of intercession those people who are ordered to the النار, and then intercession is made لذا that they do not enter it. The Narration Of ‘Abdullah Ibn ‘عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab (here, parts are missing from the original manuscript حتى the next الفصل). In another narration, Ibn ‘Abbaas x related that الرسول ﷺ of الله ig said, “My intercession is for the perpetrators, from my nation, of the major الذنوب.” At-Tabaraanee related this in his Mo‘jam Al-Kabeer. ‘Abdullah ibn ‘عمر . reported that النبي ﷺ @: said, “I was given a choice between intercession and between half of my nation being in الجنة, and 1 chose intercession, لأن it is more comprehensive and more sufficient. Do you see that it is for the Muttageen (the pious, the الصالح, etc.)? No, but rather it is for the stained, the sinners.” (أحمد) Tbn Abeo Ad-Dunyaa related the same through a different chain. And I found the same in Kifaab AlAluwvaal, And Al-Baihagee related it in Al-Ba'th Wen-Nushoor through a different chain of narrators. Leave not one of the disbelievers on the earth!?l ‘The Narration Of ‘Abdullah Ibn Mas’ood 2 We have hitherto mentioned the narration of ‘Ilgimah about the Haid (the Basin), the station of praise and glory, and in it, the intercession is mentioned as well. 586 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations The Narration Of ‘Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn Abee “‘Ageel 2s “Abdur-Rahmaan ibn Abee ‘Aqeel a said, “I went with a delegation to النبي ﷺ #8, and عندما we reached him, we made our (camels) kneel at (his) door. There was no person who was more despised by us than the man we were entering upon, and عندما we came out, there was no man more beloved to us than the man we had just entered upon, One among them (among the delegation) said, ‘O Messenger of الله, you asked your Lord for the dominion of Sulaimaan?’ الرسول ﷺ of الله ## laughed and then said, ‘Perhaps to fulfill your needs is better with الله than the dominion of Sulaimaan. Verily, الله did not send a النبي except that He gave him a الدعاء. Among them was he who used it for the الدنيا, and he was given it. Among them was he who supplicated against his people بعد they disobeyed him, and they were destroyed by it. And indeed, الله gave me a الدعاء, and I saved it with my Lord: intercession for my nation on the Day of البعث.” (Related by Al-Baihagee) The chain is Ghareeb and the الحديث is Ghareeb. The Narration Of The Leader Of The المؤمنون, ‘Uthmaan Ibn ‘Affaan <&: The Intercessors On The Day Of البعث Are The الأنبياء, Then The العلماء, And Then The Martyrs The Narration Of ‘Alee Ibn Abee Taalib PS Harb ibn Shuraih Al-Bazzaar said, “I said to Abee Ja’far محمد ﷺ ibn ‘Alee, ‘Do you see this intercession that the people of Iraq are talking about, is it true?’ قال, ‘Intercession of what?’ I said, ‘The intercession of محمد ﷺ #5. قال, ‘Yes, by الله, it is true. By الله, my uncle, محمد ﷺ ibn ‘Alee ibn Al- الكتاب of The End : Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 587 Hanafiyyah related to me from ‘Alee 4 that الرسول ﷺ of الله #8 said: 1 will intercede for my nation حتى my Lord ‘Azza Wa-Jall (To Him belongs Might and Majesty) calls me and says ~ are you pleased, O محمد ﷺ? Then I will say: My Lord, I am pleased.” (Related by أبو بكر Al-Bazzaar) Al-Bazzaar then said, “We do not know of this being related except by this chain.” The Narration Of ‘Aouf Ibn Maalik “Aout ibn Maalik Al-Ashja’ee 4 related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 28 said, “A comer from my Lord came to me in the night, giving me the choice between half of my nation entering الجنة and between intercession. And I chose intercession... 1 make those who are present to bear witness — that my intercession is for he, from my nation, who dies, not associating any partner whatsoever with, الله.” (Related by أبو بكر ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) Jn another narration, ‘Aouf ibn Maalik related that النبي ﷺ # said, “Jibreel 3421 came to me from my Lord, and gave me a choice between two qualities: that half of my nation enter الجنة or the intercession, and I chose the intercession.” (Related by Ya’qoob ibn. Sufyaa) Al-Baihagee and Hammaad ibn Zaid both related it through their own respective chains. The Narration Of Ka’ab Ibn ‘Ujrah 2% Ka’ab ibn ‘Ujrah & said, “I said, ‘O Messenger of الله, the intercession, the intercession.’ Then he # said, ‘My intercession is for the perpetrators, from my nation, of the major الذنوب.” (Related by Al-Baihagee)
